priority_order: Implement Y = torch::matmul(W, X) and Z = torch::matmul(B.transpose(0,1), X), then a single custom CUDA kernel accumulating Y[i,j] += sum_r A[i,r] * Z[r,j] in-place with rank loop fully unrolled; use a 2D grid over rows and 128-column tiles where each thread computes about 8 contiguous columns.
Benchmark low-risk tile mutations of the fused update kernel: 64/128/256 column tiles, 4/8 columns per thread, 128/256 threads per block, with coalesced accesses to Z/Y and cached per-row A values.
If one tile shape wins only for part of d range, add simple shape-aware dispatch for d buckets around 3584, 4096, and 4608 while keeping a generic fallback.
llm_advice.rationale: The baseline is correct but only matches ATen because it still uses unfused tensor operations. The safest next optimization is to retain cuBLAS for the dominant W @ X GEMM and B.T @ X projection, then fuse only the fixed-rank-16 update into Y. A 128-column tile kernel with each thread computing multiple contiguous columns is the highest-priority baseline because it exploits rank-16 unrolling, coalesced Y/Z accesses, and avoids materializing A @ Z.
risk_controls: Keep the existing ATen baseline as fallback and never promote a candidate unless correctness passes for all tested d values.
Do not replace the large W @ X GEMM with custom CUDA; use ATen/cuBLAS for W @ X.
Do not materialize the full A @ Z tensor and do not add a separate final add kernel.
Use at::cuda::getCurrentCUDAStream() for the custom kernel and check cudaGetLastError after launch.
Require bounds checks for tail rows and columns because d is runtime-selected.
Validate tensor dtype, CUDA device, dimensions, and contiguity handling; call contiguous only when needed and preserve correctness for non-contiguous inputs.
Keep scalar-safe loads/stores first; only add float4/vectorized variants behind alignment and d-multiple checks.
stop_conditions: Stop the first fused-rank candidate if it fails correctness, has launch errors, or regresses aggregate speed below the ATen baseline.
Stop exploring custom rank-update tile variants if the fused kernel contributes negligible improvement across all d values after 3 to 5 low-risk tile mutations.
Reject any variant that improves one shape but causes a material regression on another unless shape-aware dispatch removes the regression.
Do not proceed to custom full GEMM unless profiling unexpectedly shows cuBLAS is not used or is pathological.

notes: Use ATen/cuBLAS for the d x d GEMM.
Explore kernels that fuse Y += A @ T for rank 16.
